report_metadata.step_description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=6 (document_category="TAL"). TAL files do not require chunking — run this step directly after Step 2 without needing Steps 3, 4, or 5. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those entries with document_category of exactly "TAL" (Technical Asset List). These are the spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) identified in Step 1. For each TAL document, use the proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet process as the extraction guide. Each spreadsheet entry from Step 2 includes worksheet_count (total sheets), worksheets_to_analyse (non-empty sheets), and a worksheets array listing each sheet name with its row_count and column_count — use these to plan how many extraction passes are needed. For each non-empty worksheet: identify the header row, map column names to standard asset fields (Asset ID, Serial Number, Asset Type, Description, Location, Address, Feeder, Voltage Level, Capacity Rating, Manufacturer, Model, Condition Rating, Status, Installation Date, Last Inspection Date), and extract every data row as a structured asset record. Include source_sheet and source_document in every record. Produce a consolidated output with all asset_records across all sheets and all TAL files. OUTPUT FILE: The agent saves a dedicated AssetExtract_{timestamp}.json file to the job output directory. This file contains: process_id, timestamp, source_documents (list of spreadsheet filenames), total_assets (integer), and asset_records (flat array of all extracted asset records across all sheets and all TAL files). This file is stored in state as asset_extraction and is available for use by Step 7 (report consolidation) and any subsequent steps.
report_metadata.validation_criteria: Must return a JSON object with "total_files" integer and "extractions" array. Each extraction entry must include "filename", "total_sections", "relevant_sections", and "step_extraction" containing "asset_records" (array), "total_assets" (integer), and "source_documents" (array). Every asset_record must have at minimum: asset_id or serial_number (at least one non-null), asset_type, and source_sheet. Only documents with document_category "TAL" must be processed — no PDF or Design Brief files. The response must also include asset_extract_file (path to AssetExtract_*.json) and asset_extract.total_assets (integer count of all extracted asset records).

files_read.description: Output file produced by the agent

files_output.description: Validation test report generated by step validator

execution_context.prompt:
## STEP NAME
Extract Asset Spreadsheet Data

## STEP DESCRIPTION
NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=6 (document_category="TAL"). TAL files do not require chunking — run this step directly after Step 2 without needing Steps 3, 4, or 5. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those entries with document_category of exactly "TAL" (Technical Asset List). These are the spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) identified in Step 1. For each TAL document, use the proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet process as the extraction guide. Each spreadsheet entry from Step 2 includes worksheet_count (total sheets), worksheets_to_analyse (non-empty sheets), and a worksheets array listing each sheet name with its row_count and column_count — use these to plan how many extraction passes are needed. For each non-empty worksheet: identify the header row, map column names to standard asset fields (Asset ID, Serial Number, Asset Type, Description, Location, Address, Feeder, Voltage Level, Capacity Rating, Manufacturer, Model, Condition Rating, Status, Installation Date, Last Inspection Date), and extract every data row as a structured asset record. Include source_sheet and source_document in every record. Produce a consolidated output with all asset_records across all sheets and all TAL files. OUTPUT FILE: The agent saves a dedicated AssetExtract_{timestamp}.json file to the job output directory. This file contains: process_id, timestamp, source_documents (list of spreadsheet filenames), total_assets (integer), and asset_records (flat array of all extracted asset records across all sheets and all TAL files). This file is stored in state as asset_extraction and is available for use by Step 7 (report consolidation) and any subsequent steps.

## VALIDATION CRITERIA
Must return a JSON object with "total_files" integer and "extractions" array. Each extraction entry must include "filename", "total_sections", "relevant_sections", and "step_extraction" containing "asset_records" (array), "total_assets" (integer), and "source_documents" (array). Every asset_record must have at minimum: asset_id or serial_number (at least one non-null), asset_type, and source_sheet. Only documents with document_category "TAL" must be processed — no PDF or Design Brief files. The response must also include asset_extract_file (path to AssetExtract_*.json) and asset_extract.total_assets (integer count of all extracted asset records).

## YOUR TASK
Generate and execute structured test cases against the agent's output. Use the FULL FILE CONTENTS (when provided) as your primary evidence. Cross-reference every item between input and output files. For each test case, document: the input data examined, the expected output, the actual output found, step-by-step execution notes of how you validated, and your final reasoning. Respond with the JSON test report.
